heading: Protection of land use as religious exercise
label: 2000cc Protection of land use as religious exercise
notes: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–274, § 1114 Stat. 803Sept. 22, 2000“This Act [enacting this chapter and amending sections 1988, 2000bb–2 and 2000bb–3 of this title] may be cited as the ‘Religious Land Use and Institutionalized Persons Act of 2000’ ”.
amendments: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–274, § 1114 Stat. 803Sept. 22, 2000“This Act [enacting this chapter and amending sections 1988, 2000bb–2 and 2000bb–3 of this title] may be cited as the ‘Religious Land Use and Institutionalized Persons Act of 2000’ ”.

children.label: A
children.text: is in furtherance of a compelling governmental interest; and

children.label: B
children.text: is the least restrictive means of furthering that compelling governmental interest.

children.label: A
children.text: the substantial burden is imposed in a program or activity that receives Federal financial assistance, even if the burden results from a rule of general applicability;

children.label: B
children.text: the substantial burden affects, or removal of that substantial burden would affect, commerce with foreign nations, among the several States, or with Indian tribes, even if the burden results from a rule of general applicability; or

children.label: C
children.text: the substantial burden is imposed in the implementation of a land use regulation or system of land use regulations, under which a government makes, or has in place formal or informal procedures or practices that permit the government to make, individualized assessments of the proposed uses for the property involved.

children.heading: Equal terms
children.label: 1 Equal terms
children.text: No government shall impose or implement a land use regulation in a manner that treats a religious assembly or institution on less than equal terms with a nonreligious assembly or institution.

children.heading: Nondiscrimination
children.label: 2 Nondiscrimination
children.text: No government shall impose or implement a land use regulation that discriminates against any assembly or institution on the basis of religion or religious denomination.

children.label: A
children.text: totally excludes religious assemblies from a jurisdiction; or

children.label: B
children.text: unreasonably limits religious assemblies, institutions, or structures within a jurisdiction.
